questions.prompt: Complete: "Last year we ___ in Sousse on holiday."
questions.explanation: We takes were in the past: Last year we were in Sousse. Was goes with I/he/she/it, are is the present, and been is the past participle, not the simple past.

questions.prompt: Complete: "I ___ very tired after the match."
questions.explanation: I takes was in the past: I was very tired. Were is for you/we/they, am is the present, and been needs an auxiliary like have before it.

questions.prompt: Complete: "She ___ the window because it was cold."
questions.explanation: close already ends in -e, so we add only -d: closed. We never write "closeed" (double e) or "closd" (missing e). "close" alone is the base form, not the past.

questions.prompt: Complete: "They ___ for the exam all weekend."
questions.explanation: study ends in consonant + y, so the y becomes -ied: studied. "studyed" keeps the y wrongly, "studed" drops a letter, and "studies" is the present he/she/it form.

questions.prompt: Complete: "The taxi ___ in front of the house."
questions.explanation: stop has one short vowel + one consonant, so we double the p before -ed: stopped. "stoped" forgets to double, and "stopt"/"stopd" are not English spellings.

questions.prompt: Complete: "He ___ at school yesterday; he was ill at home."
questions.explanation: He takes was, so the negative is wasn't: He wasn't at school. Weren't is for you/we/they, didn't is for action verbs (not to be), and isn't is the present.
